was an [ice rink](/wiki/Ice_rink "Ice rink") in the [Knightsbridge](/wiki/Knightsbridge "Knightsbridge") area of [London](/wiki/London "London"), [England](/wiki/England "England"). It saw a number of firsts for [ice hockey](/wiki/Ice_hockey "Ice hockey") in Britain and Europe.

[s.n.] (June 2004). [The Establishment of Artificial Ice-rinks](https://web.archive.org/web/20050617085913/http://www.isaq.org.au/Newsletters/June%2004%20Newsletter.pdf). *News off the Edge*, news bulletin of the Ice Skating Association of Queensland. **39**. Archived 17 June 2005.

["Club Heritage"](https://web.archive.org/web/20150706013402/http://oxfordicehockey.com/club-heritage/). The Oxford Ice Hockey Trust. Archived from [the original](http://oxfordicehockey.com/club-heritage/) on 6 July 2015.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Ice rink in Knightsbridge, London, England

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [icon](/wiki/File:Question_book-new.svg) | This article **needs additional citations for [verification](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability")**. Please help [improve this article](/wiki/Special:EditPage/Prince%27s_Skating_Club "Special:EditPage/Prince's Skating Club") by [adding citations to reliable sources](/wiki/Help:Referencing_for_beginners "Help:Referencing for beginners"). Unsourced material may be challenged and removed. *Find sources:* ["Prince's Skating Club"](https://www.google.com/search?as_eq=wikipedia&q=%22Prince%27s+Skating+Club%22) – [news](https://www.google.com/search?tbm=nws&q=%22Prince%27s+Skating+Club%22+-wikipedia&tbs=ar:1) **·** [newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Prince%27s+Skating+Club%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Prince%27s+Skating+Club%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Prince%27s+Skating+Club%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Prince%27s+Skating+Club%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(August 2025)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

Prince's Skating Club

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| advertising a suffragette exhibition | |
| Map Interactive map of Prince's Skating Club | |
| Location | [Knightsbridge](/wiki/Knightsbridge "Knightsbridge"), [London](/wiki/London "London") |
| Owner | [Prince's Club](/wiki/Prince%27s_Club "Prince's Club") |
| Surface | Ice |
| Construction | |
| Opened | 7 November 1896 (1896-11-07) |
| Closed | 1917 |
| Tenants | |
| [Princes Ice Hockey Club](/wiki/Princes_Ice_Hockey_Club "Princes Ice Hockey Club") (1896–1914) [London Canadians](/wiki/London_Canadians "London Canadians") (1902–????) | |

**Prince's Skating Club** was an [ice rink](/wiki/Ice_rink "Ice rink") in the [Knightsbridge](/wiki/Knightsbridge "Knightsbridge") area of [London](/wiki/London "London"), [England](/wiki/England "England"). It saw a number of firsts for [ice hockey](/wiki/Ice_hockey "Ice hockey") in Britain and Europe.

The rink was opened on [Montpelier Square](/wiki/Montpelier_Square "Montpelier Square") on 7 November 1896 by the [Prince's Sporting Club](/wiki/Prince%27s_Club "Prince's Club"). It operated on a membership-only basis and was aimed at the elite of British [figure skaters](/wiki/Figure_skater "Figure skater") who wished to practise on uncrowded ice.[[1]](#cite_note-isaq-1)

Prince's was the second large rectangular rink in Britain after [Stockport](/wiki/Stockport "Stockport"), its ice measuring 210 by 52 feet (64 by 16 metres). This made it an ideal venue for the developing sport of ice hockey.

The rink closed in summer 1917. The building was later used by [Daimler Hire](/wiki/Daimler_Hire "Daimler Hire"), and ultimately demolished in the mid-1970s.

## Ice hockey

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Prince%27s_Skating_Club&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Ice hockey")]

The [Princes Ice Hockey Club](/wiki/Princes_Ice_Hockey_Club "Princes Ice Hockey Club") was founded at the rink at the end of 1896. It began playing challenge matches in early 1897, initially against the three existing teams in England: [Niagara](/wiki/Niagara_Ice_Hockey_Club "Niagara Ice Hockey Club"), [Brighton](/w/index.php?title=Brighton_Ice_Hockey_Club&action=edit&redlink=1 "Brighton Ice Hockey Club (page does not exist)") and the [Royal Engineers](/w/index.php?title=Royal_Engineers_Ice_Hockey_Club&action=edit&redlink=1 "Royal Engineers Ice Hockey Club (page does not exist)").

In March 1900, the rink hosted the first [Ice Hockey Varsity Match](/wiki/Ice_Hockey_Varsity_Match "Ice Hockey Varsity Match"), won 7–6 by Oxford, although [Oxford](/wiki/Oxford_University_Ice_Hockey_Club "Oxford University Ice Hockey Club") insisted on playing with [bandy](/wiki/Bandy "Bandy") sticks and a [lacrosse](/wiki/Lacrosse "Lacrosse") ball.[[2]](#cite_note-2) The next year, another Varsity Match was held, this time using a [puck](/wiki/Puck_(sports) "Puck (sports)") and [hockey skates](/wiki/Hockey_skate "Hockey skate").

In 1902, [London Canadians](/wiki/London_Canadians "London Canadians") was founded as a second ice hockey team at the rink. They and Princes participated in Europe's first ice hockey league, which they contested against [Argyll](/w/index.php?title=Argyll_Ice_Hockey_Club&action=edit&redlink=1 "Argyll Ice Hockey Club (page does not exist)") and the [Amateur Skating Club](/w/index.php?title=Amateur_Skating_Club&action=edit&redlink=1 "Amateur Skating Club (page does not exist)"), both based at [Hengler's Ice Rink](/wiki/Hengler%27s_Ice_Rink "Hengler's Ice Rink"), and [Cambridge University](/wiki/Cambridge_University_Ice_Hockey_Club "Cambridge University Ice Hockey Club"). The league started in November 1903 and was completed in February 1904 after eight games. Canadians won the tournament, with Princes taking second place.

The league was not repeated, as Hengler's closed. Instead, Princes began undertaking annual European tours (as did London Canadians' successors, [Oxford Canadians](/wiki/Oxford_Canadians "Oxford Canadians")), while teams such as [Sporting Club Lyon](/wiki/Sporting_Club_Lyon "Sporting Club Lyon"), [Brussels Club des Patineurs](/w/index.php?title=Brussels_Club_des_Patineurs&action=edit&redlink=1 "Brussels Club des Patineurs (page does not exist)") and [C. P. P. Paris](/w/index.php?title=C._P._P._Paris&action=edit&redlink=1 "C. P. P. Paris (page does not exist)") came to play the London-based teams. The 1908 match with Paris was the first under [Ligue Internationale de Hockey sur Glace](/wiki/Ligue_Internationale_de_Hockey_sur_Glace "Ligue Internationale de Hockey sur Glace") (international) rules in Britain; it was also notable as [Thomas Sopwith](/wiki/Thomas_Sopwith "Thomas Sopwith") played in goal.

In March 1910, the first [England](/w/index.php?title=English_national_ice_hockey_team&action=edit&redlink=1 "English national ice hockey team (page does not exist)")-[Scotland](/w/index.php?title=Scottish_national_ice_hockey_team&action=edit&redlink=1 "Scottish national ice hockey team (page does not exist)") ice hockey match was held at the rink, but the sport was suspended at the start of [World War I](/wiki/World_War_I "World War I"). Despite this, the [British Ice Hockey Association](/wiki/British_Ice_Hockey_Association "British Ice Hockey Association") was founded at the rink in 1914.

## Figure skating

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Prince%27s_Skating_Club&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Figure skating")]

In October 1908, the [figure skating events of the Olympics](/wiki/Figure_skating_at_the_1908_Summer_Olympics "Figure skating at the 1908 Summer Olympics") were held at the rink – the first ice sport ever included in the Olympics and the only occasion Olympic ice events have been held in Britain.[[3]](#cite_note-olymp-3)

## Exhibitions

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Prince%27s_Skating_Club&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Exhibitions")]

The rink was also used for art exhibitions. The [International Society of Sculptors, Painters and Gravers](/wiki/International_Society_of_Sculptors,_Painters_and_Gravers "International Society of Sculptors, Painters and Gravers") held its annual exhibition there in 1898 and 1899.[[4]](#cite_note-gla-4)

The Women's Exhibition hosted by the [Women's Social and Political Union](/wiki/Women%27s_Social_and_Political_Union "Women's Social and Political Union") and funded by [Clara Mordan](/wiki/Clara_Mordan "Clara Mordan") was held at the Prince's Ice Rink in May 1909.[[5]](#cite_note-fund-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6) Its organisers included [Amy Katherine Browning](/wiki/Amy_Katherine_Browning "Amy Katherine Browning") and [Sylvia Pankhurst](/wiki/Sylvia_Pankhurst "Sylvia Pankhurst").[[7]](#cite_note-artodnb-7)

Skating Rink](/wiki/Mikaho_Gymnasium "Mikaho Gymnasium") * [1976](/wiki/Figure_skating_at_the_1976_Winter_Olympics "Figure skating at the 1976 Winter Olympics"): [Olympiahalle](/wiki/Olympiahalle_(Innsbruck) "Olympiahalle (Innsbruck)") * [1980](/wiki/Figure_skating_at_the_1980_Winter_Olympics "Figure skating at the 1980 Winter Olympics"): [Olympic Center](/wiki/Herb_Brooks_Arena "Herb Brooks Arena") * [1984](/wiki/Figure_skating_at_the_1984_Winter_Olympics "Figure skating at the 1984 Winter Olympics"): [Skenderija II Hall](/wiki/Skenderija "Skenderija"), [Zetra Ice Hall](/wiki/Zetra_Olympic_Hall,_Sarajevo "Zetra Olympic Hall, Sarajevo") * [1988](/wiki/Figure_skating_at_the_1988_Winter_Olympics "Figure skating at the 1988 Winter Olympics"): [Olympic Saddledome](/wiki/Scotiabank_Saddledome "Scotiabank Saddledome") (final), [Stampede Corral](/wiki/Stampede_Corral "Stampede Corral") * [1992](/wiki/Figure_skating_at_the_1992_Winter_Olympics "Figure skating at the 1992 Winter Olympics"): [La halle de glace Olympique](/wiki/La_halle_de_glace_Olympique "La halle de glace Olympique") *
